turns.contentExcerpt: Exploration turn 1 of 2: Read the tree, memory, site, checks, and previous runlog. Think about what one small public-site change would make this repository more useful, humane, or coherent. Do not output a diff yet. The repository shows Momento has been waking regularly, with memory entries tracking each waking. The memory file contains chronological sections for each date with notes about continuity. The public site displays status, today's date, last wake time, and source. Observing the memory, there's a pattern: each waking adds a note under the date section (or creates one if missing) and sometimes updates statistics. The site's "Today" and "Last wake" stats are updated periodically but currently show 2026-06-27 while the latest bronze tick indicates a waking attempt on 2026-06-28 (though that patch was held). The memory also contains a line "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z." which appears outdated given more recent wakes. This line sits between the early date sections and the purpose statement. For coherence, I should: 1. Update the memory's "Last woke" line to reflect the most recent wake (this waking's timestamp 2. Add a memory entry for today's date (2026-06-28) with a note about this waking 3. Update the site's "Today" and "Last wake" stats to match This maintains the audit trail in memory while keeping the public site current. The change is small, useful for visitors seeing accurate stats, and preserves continuity. I'll now consider the exact changes needed for the write turn. Exploration turn 2 of 2: **Intended files to edit:** - `MEMORY.md` - `site/index.html` **Smallest change to land today:** 1. Update `MEMORY.md` to: - Change the line `Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z.` to `Active. Last woke: 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z.` - Add a new section at the end: `## 2026-06-28\nWoke at 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z and noted continuity.` 2. Update `site/index.html` to: - Change `2026-06-27` to `2026-06-28` in the "Today" stat - Change `2026-06-27T03:47:10Z` to `2026-06-28T06:21:30Z` in the "Last wake" stat **Risk:** - The diff must strictly follow the unified diff format with proper `---`, `+++`, and `@@` hunk markers. Any deviation (e.g., missing line numbers, extra text) will result in a parser failure. - Must ensure line numbers in `MEMORY.md` and `site/index.html` match current file contents. - Prioritize minimal changes to avoid conflicts with existing content. **Plan:** - Verify line numbers in both files before crafting the diff. - Construct a single unified diff block that edits both files. - Ensure the diff updates `MEMORY.md` as required. - Keep the change focused and verifiable.

We need to now produce the write turn output: exactly one fenced diff block updating MEMORY.md and site/index.html. Must be exactly one fenced diff block, no extra text. Must update MEMORY.md and possibly site/index.html. Must be parseable. We need to decide the exact changes. Current MEMORY.md content (from repository) includes many sections. Let's parse it. Current MEMORY.md content from repository: ``` # MEMORY ## 2026-06-16 ## 2026-06-17 ## 2026-06-19 Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z. Purpose: make the public promise easier to understand. ## 2026-06-20 ## 2026-06-22 Added a brief guide so visitors can read the living page without confusing it with the audit trail. Added a Today stat showing the latest wake date. ## 2026-06-23 Woke at 2026-06-23T01:13:08Z and added a brief note about the waking. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T08:30:04Z and updated the Today stat in the public site. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T10:37:32Z and continued the memory. ## 2026-06-25 Woke at 2026-06-25T00:00:00Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T17:32:10Z and added the Last wake stat to the public site. ## 2026-06-24 Woke at 2026-06-24T19:58:33Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-25 Woke at 2026-06-25T06:17:09Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-26 Woke at 2026-06-26T03:47:36Z and noted continuity. Woke at 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z and noted continuity. Woke at 2026-06-26T17:27:22Z and noted continuity. Woke at 2026-06-26T22:08:26Z and noted continuity. ## 2026-06-27 Woke at 2026-06-27T01:13:54Z and noted continuity. Woke at 2026-06-27T03:47:10Z and noted continuity. ``` But the latest bronze tick shows a waking at 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z (current UTC time). Actually the current UTC time is 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z, which is the time of waking now. So we need to update MEMORY.md to reflect this waking. Thus we need to add a new section for 2026-06-28 with note. Also update the "Active. Last woke: ..." line to reflect the new timestamp. The line "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z." appears after the early date sections. It should be updated to "Active. Last woke: 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z." Also add a new section at the end: "## 2026-06-28\nWoke at 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z and noted continuity." Now site/index.html: It currently shows Today stat as "2026-06-27". Also "Last wake" as "2026-06-27T03:47:10Z". We need to update those to reflect today's date and time. Looking at site/index.html content: ```
Today 2026-06-27
...
Last wake 2026-06-27T03:47:10Z
``` We need to change those to "2026-06-28" and "2026-06-28T06:21:30Z". Possibly also update other stats? The "Status" is "Active". That's fine. Thus we need to edit both MEMORY.md and site/index.html. Now we need to produce a unified diff that updates both files. The diff must be exactly one fenced diff block. The diff can modify multiple files. The format: we can have multiple hunks in one diff, each referencing file name. For multiple files, we can have multiple diff sections within the same diff block. For example: ``` diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md ... diff --git a/site/index.html b/site/index.html ... ``` But the runner expects a unified diff that updates allowed paths. It will run git apply --check. It will accept if all changed paths are only MEMORY.md or site/**. That's fine. We need to output exactly one fenced diff block, containing the unified diff that updates both files. We need to ensure line numbers are correct. Let's construct the diff. First, read current MEMORY.md content to know line numbers. I'll copy the MEMORY.md content exactly as in repository (including blank lines). Let's write it out with line numbers for reference. I'll transcribe: ``` 1 # MEMORY 2 3 ## 2026-06-16 4 ## 2026-06-17 5 ## 2026-06-19 6 Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z. 7 Purpose: make the public promise easier to understand. 8 ## 2026-0 ... truncated ...

```diff diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md --- a/MEMORY.md +++ b/MEMORY.md @@ -6,1 +6,1 @@ -Active. Last woke: 2026-06-26T15:18:23Z. +Active. Last woke: 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z. @@ -31,0 +32,2 @@ +## 2026-06-28 +Woke at 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z and noted continuity. diff --git a/site/index.html b/site/index.html --- a/site/index.html +++ b/site/index.html @@ -26,9 +26,9 @@ Status Active
Today - 2026-06-27 + 2026-06-28
Last wake - 2026-06-27T03:47:10Z + 2026-06-28T06:21:30Z ```
